text.title: Chroma LangChain Integration
text.summary: LangChain Chroma integration patterns: creating from documents, loading existing collections, and searching with scores.

example.code:
from langchain_community.vectorstores import Chroma from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
embeddings = OpenAIEmbeddings() # uses OPENAI_API_KEY
# Create from documents vectorstore = Chroma.from_documents( documents=docs, embedding=embeddings, persist_directory="./chroma_db", collection_name="my_collection", )
# Load existing collection vectorstore = Chroma( persist_directory="./chroma_db", embedding_function=embeddings, collection_name="my_collection", )
# Similarity search with scores results = vectorstore.similarity_search_with_score( query="search query", k=10, filter={"source": "file.pdf"}, ) for doc, score in results: print(f"Score: {1 - score:.3f} | {doc.page_content[:80]}")
example.note: LangChain also returns distances. Apply 1 - score for cosine similarity. Use the filter kwarg to pass Chroma where-clause dicts.

example.code:
import chromadb from chromadb.utils import embedding_functions import os
client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")
openai_ef = embedding_functions.OpenAIEmbeddingFunction( api_key=os.environ["OPENAI_API_KEY"], model_name="text-embedding-3-small", )
collection = client.get_or_create_collection( name="auto_embed", embedding_function=openai_ef, )
# Add raw text — Chroma calls OpenAI automatically collection.add(ids=["1", "2"], documents=["First doc", "Second doc"])
# Query with raw text results = collection.query(query_texts=["search query"], n_results=5)
example.note: Auto-embedding is convenient but couples the collection to a specific API key at creation time. Prefer explicit embeddings for production pipelines.
